title: Pounce - Dependency Guard Dashboard
description: Pounce is a Codex-native dependency guard dashboard for exact release vetting, hook enforcement, feed trust, and workspace protection state.
headings: Dependency changes get checked before they become repo state.
Guardrails stay attached to the turn.
Test the decision surface without leaving the page.
Signals stay explainable when the network is not perfect.
Recent verdicts read like evidence, not mystery state.
Local-first security, shaped for agent workflows.

links.text: Supply-chain response center Local guardrails for npm/PyPI installs, manifest edits, and feed trust.

textLines: [a] Supply-chain response center Local guardrails for npm/PyPI installs, manifest edits, and feed trust.
[span] Local guardrails for npm/PyPI installs, manifest edits, and feed trust.
[h1] Dependency changes get checked before they become repo state.
[p] Pounce vets exact releases, watches shell-time install commands, snapshots dependency files, and shows feed trust directly inside the agent workflow.
[h2] Guardrails stay attached to the turn.
[p] Install checks, dependency-file snapshots, and dashboard state share one workspace view so risky package edits do not drift out of context.
[span] Built for demos, README proof points, and chat-native status snapshots.
[h2] Test the decision surface without leaving the page.
[p] The interface models Pounce's release workflow: exact packages pass through allow, warn, and block outcomes with inline state changes.
[p] Use package specs such as jose@6.2.2, axios@1.15.0, or plain-crypto-js@4.2.1.
[p] Exact npm specs use name@version. Exact PyPI specs use name==version.
[span] Submit an exact release to populate this verdict panel.
[p] Three stages keep the dependency story attached to the agent turn.
[span] Snapshot dependency manifests and lockfiles before tools run.
[span] Inspect install commands and block known bad or non-exact specs.
[span] Diff dependency files against the snapshot and flag unexplained edits.
[h2] Signals stay explainable when the network is not perfect.
[p] Pounce separates confirmed malicious-package intelligence from warning-only provenance and install mechanics, then reports the active source in the dashboard.
[span] HTTPS-only, no redirects, capped response body.
[span] GitHub malware advisories and OSV-derived records.
[span] Offline fallback for demos and first-run coverage.
[h2] Recent verdicts read like evidence, not mystery state.
[p] The dashboard keeps workspace protection, feed source, and package verdicts visible together so a reviewer can trace why a recommendation changed.
[h2] Local-first security, shaped for agent workflows.
[p] The page now matches the product story: exact release checks, hook enforcement, feed trust, and chat-native status in one visible surface.
[span] Pounce protects dependency changes before they settle into repo state.
